title: Replace broken PDF download with Save as HTML

requirements.summary: Replace the broken PDF download functionality with a client-side HTML export that generates a fully self-contained .html file containing all resume data, inline styles, and base64-encoded profile photo for offline viewing and browser-based printing
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the download button triggers a browser file download of a file named 'resume.html'.
The downloaded .html file is fully self-contained — all styles are inlined and the profile photo (if present) is embedded as a base64 data URL, requiring no external resources.
Opening the downloaded .html file in any modern browser displays the complete, formatted resume matching the live preview layout (header, contact info, photo, all sections).
The downloaded HTML can be printed or saved as PDF directly from the browser's print dialog without any missing content.
No blank page or infinite loading state occurs when clicking the download button.
The old window.print() / html2canvas PDF generation code in useDownloadPDF.ts is removed and replaced with the HTML export logic.
The download button label is updated to reflect 'Download as HTML' or 'Save as HTML' to set correct user expectations.

file_operations.description: Replace the entire PDF generation logic (window.print, html2canvas, DOM cloning with style injection) with HTML export logic. Implement a function that serializes the complete resume data into a self-contained HTML string with all CSS inlined (including theme colors, fonts, layout rules from index.css and Tailwind). Convert any profile photo blob URL to a base64 data URL before embedding. Generate a Blob with type 'text/html', create an object URL, and trigger download via a programmatic anchor click with download='resume.html'. Return a downloadHTML function from the hook.

Update the download button in the header to call the new downloadHTML function from useDownloadPDF instead of the old downloadPDF. Change the button label or aria-label to 'Download as HTML' or 'Save as HTML' to reflect the new functionality and set correct user expectations.
